name: Lasers & Feelings
edition: v1.4 (2013, John Harper — written as a Doubleclicks tribute)
license: Game format CC BY 4.0 — text and mechanics © 2013 John Harper, used under the CC BY 4.0 grant explicitly published on the one-page rules. This ruleset file is a clean-room re-implementation of the mechanics for the Marinara-RPG framework; no verbatim play examples or unique flavor prose are reproduced.
summary: One-page space-adventure RPG. Each character has a single stat (Number, 2–5). LASERS rolls succeed under Number; FEELINGS rolls succeed over Number; rolling exactly Number is LASER FEELINGS — counts as a success AND grants the player an honest GM answer.

dice.notation: Pd6 stance=LASERS|FEELINGS vs Number (1–4 dice typical)

stances.label: LASERS
stances.description: Science, technology, cold rationality, calm precise action.

stances.label: FEELINGS
stances.description: Diplomacy, intuition, seduction, wild passionate action.

exactMatch.name: LASER FEELINGS
exactMatch.effect: Ask the GM a question and they answer honestly. The die also counts as a success toward the outcome tier.

outcomeTiers.label: miss
outcomeTiers.narration: It goes wrong. GM says how things get worse.

outcomeTiers.label: barely
outcomeTiers.narration: You barely manage it. GM inflicts a complication, harm, or cost.

outcomeTiers.label: good
outcomeTiers.narration: You do it well. Good job!

outcomeTiers.label: critical
outcomeTiers.narration: Critical success. GM tells you an extra effect you get.

attributes.name: Number
attributes.description: Your single stat. High Number = better at LASERS (science, reason). Low Number = better at FEELINGS (intuition, passion). Choose somewhere in the middle if you want balance; choose an extreme if you want to specialize.

skills.name: Help
skills.description: Helping marker. When another player rolls and you describe how you assist, mark this and roll a Help check; on success the originator gains +1d to their pool. Tracked here as a single skill slot to satisfy the framework's per-character skill list; the helping rule itself is GM-resolved per the L&F one-page rules.

diceTagFormat.template: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=lasers-and-feelings, stance={stance}, stat=Number, statValue={statValue}, pool={pool}, dice=[{diceCsv}], successes={successes}, exactMatches={exactMatches}, tier={tier}{narrationHookPart}]
diceTagFormat.example: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=lasers-and-feelings, stance=lasers, stat=Number, statValue=4, pool=3, dice=[1,4,5], successes=2, exactMatches=1, tier=good, narrationHook=laser_feelings]
